mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
Use the Google Cloud CLI to create a GKE cluster. Replace `YOUR_PROJECT_ID` and `YOUR_REGION` with your actual project ID and desired region.
```bash
gcloud container clusters create my-cluster --project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID --region=YOUR_REGION --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3
```

steps.title: Connect kubectl to the Cluster
steps.description:
After the cluster is created, configure kubectl to use the new cluster.
```bash
gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-cluster --region=YOUR_REGION --project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID
```

steps.title: Verify Cluster Setup
steps.description:
Check the cluster information and node status to ensure everything is running correctly.
```bash
kubectl cluster-info
kubectl get nodes
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration: Enable Autoscaling
steps.description:
Enable cluster autoscaler for node pools to automatically adjust the number of nodes based on workload.
```bash
gcloud container clusters update my-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes=1 --max-nodes=5 --region=YOUR_REGION
```

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
To remove the GKE cluster and all associated resources, run:
```bash
gcloud container clusters delete my-cluster --region=YOUR_REGION --quiet
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade GKE Cluster Version
upgrade.description:
To upgrade your GKE cluster to a new version, first check the available versions:
```bash
gcloud container get-server-config --region=YOUR_REGION
```
Then upgrade the cluster:
```bash
gcloud container clusters upgrade my-cluster --master --cluster-version=1.31 --region=YOUR_REGION
```
Backup your resources and configurations before upgrading. To rollback, you can downgrade using the same command with the previous version.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl command fails with 'context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
This usually indicates that kubectl is not configured to use the correct context. Run:
```bash
gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-cluster --region=YOUR_REGION --project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID
```

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
Check the node conditions using:
```bash
kubectl describe nodes
```
Look for any issues in the events section. Common causes include insufficient resources or network issues.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description: This may indicate insufficient resources in the cluster. Check the resource requests of your pods and ensure your node pool has enough capacity. You can also enable cluster autoscaling.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced improved security features and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API versions for certain resources.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node types and improved performance for large clusters.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain flags in the gcloud CLI.

resolution.summary: A successful setup includes a running GKE cluster with nodes in 'Ready' status and kubectl configured to interact with the cluster. You should also have enabled any necessary post-install configurations such as autoscaling.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and authenticated.
